_readme: Copy this entire folder, rename it (e.g. ch02), then edit this file. Delete any _readme fields before building.
title: Your Chapter Title Here
series_subtitle: A Field Guide for Frontline Folks and Their Families
cover_tagline: One short line that appears below the subtitle on the cover.

sections._note: Each section gets a numbered badge heading. 'number' is the badge number.
sections.heading: First Section Title

content.text: This is a body paragraph. Write your content here. It will wrap automatically.

content._note: PARAGRAPH with indent — use for sub-paragraphs.
BULLET LIST — items can be plain strings OR objects with a bold 'lead' word.
items: Plain bullet point — just a string

content._note: INDENTED BULLETS — use level 1 for sub-bullets under a main bullet.
NUMBERED LIST — auto-numbers from 1. Items can have a bold 'lead'.
CALLOUT BOX (default) — steel blue left bar, light blue background.
lines: First line — bold because bold_first is true.
Second line — empty string above creates a small gap.

content._note: CALLOUT BOX (highlight) — same as default but use for key takeaways.
CALLOUT BOX (info) — slightly different background, good for disclaimers or notes.
lines: An informational note or sidebar goes here.

content._note: CLINICAL NOTE — italic first line, green accent bar. Use for 'why this works' notes. Set clinical_only: true to include only in the Clinical PDF.
lines: Why this works: clinical explanation goes here. Keep it plain language.

content._note: DIVIDER — a thin horizontal rule between topics.

sections._note: Second section — shows the Red/Yellow/Green coloured blocks.
sections.heading: Red / Yellow / Green Example

content.text: Intro paragraph before the coloured blocks.

content._note: GREEN BLOCK — thick green left bar.
lines: Example phrase: “I’m green. We can talk about it.”

content._note: YELLOW BLOCK — thick amber left bar.
lines: Example phrase: “I’m yellow. Can we go slow?”

content._note: RED BLOCK — thick red left bar.
lines: RED  —  “Not Safe to Talk Right Now”
Example phrase: “I’m in the red. I need 30 minutes.”

content._note: BLUE BOX — used for Blow Up / Shut Down style contrast boxes.

content.text: This paragraph appears in BOTH the Standard and Clinical PDF.
This Subheading Only Appears in the Clinical PDF
This paragraph only appears in the Clinical PDF because clinical_only is true.
This paragraph only appears in the Standard PDF because standard_only is true.

worksheets._note: WORKSHEET with question groups — each group has a label and N text fields.
worksheets.title: First Worksheet Title
worksheets.intro: Brief instructions for how to complete this worksheet.

question_groups.label: First question group label
question_groups.hint: italic hint text shown under the label

worksheets.closing_note: Keep this somewhere private and update it as needed.

worksheets._note: WORKSHEET with numbered prompts — repair-script style with large fill fields.
worksheets.title: Second Worksheet Title (Script Style)
worksheets.intro: Work through each step and fill in your responses.

numbered_prompts.step: 3.  One concrete change you’re willing to try:

worksheets.closing_note: Keep this somewhere private and update it as needed.

closing._note: Optional — if omitted, a default closing is used.
paragraphs: First closing paragraph. Speak directly to the reader here.
Second closing paragraph. Acknowledge what they’ve done by reading this.
closing.highlight: The key closing message that stands out visually in a callout box.
